requirements.text: Apply per-IP rate limiting before REST endpoint handlers perform SQLite-backed work.
requirements.acceptance: Repeated requests from one client beyond the configured allowance receive HTTP 429 without invoking the protected handler/store path.

requirements.text: Preserve existing behavior for requests under the limit.
requirements.acceptance: Existing API tests continue to pass except where new tests explicitly exercise throttling.
A throttled request returns HTTP 429, includes Retry-After, and returns a JSON body with a clear rate-limit detail.

requirements.text: Make rate limits configurable via TPSConfig.
requirements.acceptance: requests_per_minute and burst_size defaults are present, load from TOML, environment, and Hermes context config, and validate invalid values.
Implementation uses stdlib data structures plus existing FastAPI/Starlette middleware hooks; no Redis, slowapi, or new package dependency is added.

requirements.text: Exempt the health endpoint from rate limiting.
requirements.acceptance: /api/v1/health continues returning liveness information even when the same client is otherwise over limit.
Stale client entries are pruned after their request windows expire, with tests covering cleanup.
prometheus_metrics.py exposes tps_api_rate_limited_total and a helper that increments on throttled requests when Prometheus is available.

requirements.text: Log rate-limit events at DEBUG level.
requirements.acceptance: Throttled requests are logged at DEBUG with client identity and retry timing, without logging request bodies or secrets.

success_criteria: Burst requests from one IP to protected REST endpoints eventually receive HTTP 429 before hitting SQLite-backed handler logic.
Throttled responses include Retry-After and clear JSON detail, while under-limit requests preserve existing response contracts.
TPSConfig supports requests_per_minute and burst_size from defaults, TOML, environment, and context override paths with validation.
Prometheus exposes tps_api_rate_limited_total when rate-limited requests occur and Prometheus is available.
Affected API, config, rate-limiting, and Prometheus tests pass without adding external dependencies.
